topHazards.label: The coffee cup can crack or break when hot liquid is poured into it, posing a burn hazard.
The recalled children's sleepwear garments fail to meet the flammability standards for children's sleepwear, posing a risk of burn injuries to children.

sampleRecalls.title: Accompany USA Recalls Ceramic Mugs with Cork Bottoms Due to Burn Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Accompany USA Ceramic Mugs with Cork Bottoms sold as and/or given out for free as promotional products with company or other logos. The ceramic mugs are white with cork bottoms. They measure about four inches high and four inches wide.
hazards: The coffee cup can crack or break when hot liquid is poured into it, posing a burn hazard.
injuries: Accompany USA has received one report of the mug breaking after hot liquid was poured into it. No injuries have been reported.

sampleRecalls.title: Esme Recalls Children's Sleepwear Due to Violation of Federal Flammability Standards and Burn Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves four different styles of children's sleepwear garments; bunny, cherry, unicorn and shimmer sweets prints made of modal, cotton and spandex. The children's sleepwear was sold in sizes 12 months to 14 years. Children's sleepwear with the style number beginning with SF9 and a cut number of 1812, 2730…
hazards: The recalled children's sleepwear garments fail to meet the flammability standards for children's sleepwear, posing a risk of burn injuries to children.

products: Stahlwood Youngtimes Klick-Klack Rattle baby rattles

sampleRecords.nature: Multiple severe wounds and internal injuries
sampleRecords.event_description: Pedestrian struck by vehicle or object propelled by another vehicle in nonroadway area
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was helping as a forklift was getting a load unjammed in a trailer. The load shifted and crushed the employee, who suffered broken ribs and a punctured liver. The employee was hospitalized.

sampleRecords.employer: Customs and Border Protection-U.S. Border Patrol
sampleRecords.event_description: Nonroadway noncollision  fall or jump from moving vehicle
sampleRecords.final_narrative: On 7/31/2025, an employee was training to ride an ATV when it went into a draw and she went over the front of the ATV, landing on the ground. The employee sustained a fractured arm.

sampleRecords.employer: US. Department of Homeland Security - Immigration and Customs Enforcement
sampleRecords.part_of_body: Body systems and other part(s) of body

sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was participating in special response team (SRT) tryouts and sustained rhabdomyolysis performing strenuous activities.

sampleRecords.nature: Multiple severe wounds and internal injuries
sampleRecords.event_description: Pedestrian struck by vehicle in nonroadway area  unspecified
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was struck by a vehicle and suffered a non-displaced fracture of the right hip, a fractured lower vertebra, fractured right pelvis, fractured toes, and a laceration to the left kidney.

sampleRecords.employer: U.S. Customs and Border Protection - Laredo Convent Avenue Port of Entry
sampleRecords.event_description: Exposure to harmful substance through exposed tissue  unspecified
sampleRecords.final_narrative: A U.S. Customs and Border Protection officer had been inspecting the trunk of a vehicle. He collapsed and lost vital signs due to possible fentanyl exposure. There were shards of glass found in the area the officer was inspecting that tested positive for traces of fentanyl and methamphetamines. The employee was hospitalized.

sampleReleases.caption: Nicholas Toms
sampleReleases.summary: Securities and Exchange Commission v. Nicholas Toms , Civil Action No. 8:16-cv-80096 Lit. Release No. 23448 / January 20, 2016 The Securities and Exchange Commission filed a complaint in the United States District Court for the Southern District of Florida against Nicholas Toms seeking enforcement of the Commission Order requiring him to pay disgorgement of $85,918, representing profits gained by Toms' conduct described in the Commission Order, prejudgment interest to the date of the Commission
